title: Surface Area of Prisms
why: Surface area of a prism means finding the area of every face and adding them up. Opposite faces match, so you often double an area. This runs on area of a rectangle, doubling, and adding several areas. Warm those up first.

diagnostic.q: Area of a rectangle 7 long and 2 wide?
On a box, the top face and the bottom face are…

learn.heading: Match the faces, then add
learn.intro: A box has 3 pairs of matching faces: top/bottom, front/back, left/right. Find the area of one face in each pair, double it, then add all three doubled areas. Answer is in square units.
examples: Double a face: if a face is 4 × 3 = 12, the matching pair is 2 × 12 = 24.
Add the pairs: if the three doubled pairs are 24, 16, and 12, total surface area = 24 + 16 + 12 = 52 square units.

tiers.intro: Find a face area, then double it.

items.q: B. Double it for the matching pair: 2 × 8 = ___

tiers.intro: Double faces and add the pairs.

items.q: A. A face is 5 × 3. Doubled pair = 2 × (5 × 3) = ___
B. Three pairs of faces total 20, 12, and 6. Add them: ___
C. How many matching pairs of faces does a box have?

tiers.intro: Stretch: full surface area from three pairs.

items.q: A. Faces are 6×2, 6×3, and 2×3. Pairs are 2×12, 2×18, 2×6. What is the total surface area?
B. A face is 5 × 4. What is the total area of that matching pair? (square units)

exit.q: 1. A face is 4 × 5. What is the doubled pair (2 × area)?
